goal: Extract query engine into @graffio/query-language module to make the boundary explicit and compiler-enforced

steps.action: Create module scaffold. Create `modules/query-language/` directory with `package.json` (name: @graffio/query-language, version: 0.1.0, type: module, main: index.js, private: true, dependency on @graffio/functional 0.1.0, tap scripts matching functional module pattern). Create empty `src/`, `src/types/`, `type-definitions/`, `test/` directories. Create placeholder `index.js` with ABOUTME comment. Run `yarn install` from repo root to register workspace.

Rewrite metric-definition.type.js as a generated type. Replace the hand-written file at `modules/quicken-web-app/type-definitions/metric-definition.type.js` with a proper type definition: Tagged type MetricDefinition with fields `name: 'String'`, `compute: 'Object'`, `level: FieldTypes.metricLevel` (add `metricLevel: /^(position|aggregate)$/` to quicken-web-app field-types.js). Add a comment on the compute field: `// it's not an object, it's a function, I promise not to serialize it`. Run `yarn types:generate-all`. Verify the generated MetricDefinition works — `metric-registry.js` still creates MetricDefinitions with function values in the compute field (Object type skips validation). Run style validator on changed files.
steps.note: Used compute: 'Any' instead of 'Object' because validateObject rejects functions (typeof === 'object' fails for functions). 'Any' skips validation entirely.

steps.action: Move type definitions to query-language module.
